title: Driver Fatigue Detection (Trans Track)
subtitle: Funded industrial collaboration: real-time fatigue detection for commercial mining fleet dashcams.
reflection: MultiscaleTCN had to outperform a model the company was already running in production. That is a different kind of pressure than a benchmark.
Real industrial data from mining fleets is messier and more unbalanced than anything I had cleaned before. The real world does not come labeled.
I learned the difference between a demo and a service. A demo runs once. A service runs at 3am when a driver is falling asleep on a highway.
I learned to write tests for real hardware, not mock objects.
The night-hour heuristic and the driver history rule came from real incidents. Edge cases in safety systems are not edge cases. They are the whole point.

highlights: Industry project with Trans Track using real dashcam footage from active mining fleet drivers at BIB, PAMA MTBU, and SIS.
No labeled dataset existed at project start. Built a data pipeline to pull alarm events and video clips directly from the Trans Track platform.
Designed a custom model from scratch analyzing facial landmark sequences at 10 FPS, tracking eye closure, mouth movement, and head orientation to classify driver fatigue state across three classes.
Baseline was Macro F1 0.67, the score of the model already running in production. Achieved 0.78: an 11% gain.
Built risk escalation rules: alert severity increases at nighttime hours and when a driver has multiple fatigue events within 60 minutes.
Deployed as a 3-service Docker stack with full test coverage verified against the live company server.
